metadata.notes:
agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

## Reasoning

This cell is frozen at the **agent layer**. The scenario's primitive is
*orphan detection* — the daemon's startup sweep removes session transcripts
whose owning PID is gone, without a spurious `working` transition during
cleanup — but `scenarios.json` declares `requires: [headless_mode, subagents]`
and frames the recipe explicitly around a **parent/child session tree**: a
parent launches a foreground subagent via the Task tool, the recipe SIGKILLs
the parent's process group, and the invariant under test is that BOTH parent
and the orphaned *child* are swept (`parent_linked_min: 1`,
`subagent_transcripts_min: 1`). The whole point is the parent-child orphan
relationship.

pi has **no subagent primitive at all.** pi 0.70.0's README states this in
three places: "Pi ships with powerful defaults but skips features like sub
agents and plan mode" (line 23), "Sub-agents and plan mode" listed under the
not-built features (line 343), and the dedicated "**No sub-agents.** ...
Spawn pi instances via tmux, or build your own with extensions" note
(line 452). `replaydata/agents/pi/capabilities.json` records
`"subagents": false`. A pi session is therefore a single flat JSONL
transcript per running `pi` instance — there is no Task/Agent tool, no
`ParentSessionID` linkage, and no child sidechain transcript. With no
parent-child tree, there is nothing for the scenario's orphan-of-a-subagent
shape to exercise, and `parent_linked_min`/`subagent_transcripts_min` can
never be satisfied. So `agent_supports: no`.

Because `agent_supports` is `no`, `irrlicht_observes` is `n/a`: there is no
subagent tree to orphan, hence no observation to make against the scenario as
specified. (Note that the daemon's generic startup orphan sweep *does* apply
to any stale flat pi transcript whose PID is gone — that is a real,
adapter-agnostic daemon property keyed on PID liveness via
`DiscoverPIDByCWD`. But that is the single-session `session-end`/restart
behavior, not the parent-child subagent-orphan invariant this scenario tests;
the scenario-meanings "Not to be confused with" line draws exactly that
distinction.)

## Step 2.5 binary-scan guard

Before locking in `no`, I ran the keyword guard. The `pi` on PATH is a Node
shebang wrapper resolving to
`@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent@0.70.0/dist/cli.js`, so `strings $(which pi)`
is not meaningful — I grepped the real dist bundle instead:
`grep -rioE "subagent|sub-agent|spawnAgent|childSession|parentSessionId|orphan|launch.{0,3}agent|Task tool" dist/` returned **zero matches**. The
feature genuinely did not ship. Combined with the README's explicit "No
sub-agents" positioning and `subagents: false` in capabilities, the `no`
verdict is well-grounded (confidence 0.9, up from the column scan's 0.7).
caveats: The daemon's generic startup orphan sweep DOES apply to a single stale pi transcript whose PID is gone (PID-liveness via DiscoverPIDByCWD) — but that is the flat single-session restart path, not the parent-child subagent-orphan tree this scenario specifies. Re-judge only if pi adds a subagent primitive or a third-party package brings parent-child sessions into stock pi.
scenario.requires lists [headless_mode, subagents]; pi satisfies headless_mode but not subagents, so the cell is definitionally inapplicable until pi gains subagents.

sources.note: "subagents": false — no child sessions, hence no parent-child orphan tree
0.70.0 README: "No sub-agents." (line 452); "skips features like sub agents and plan mode" (line 23); "Sub-agents and plan mode" in not-built list (line 343)

Step 2.5 binary scan: grep -rioE "subagent|spawnAgent|childSession|parentSessionId|orphan|Task tool" over the real dist bundle (pi is a node-shebang wrapper, so strings on the launcher is empty) returned zero matches — no subagent code shipped in 0.70.0
Source: FilesUnderRoot{~/.pi/agent/sessions} + ExactName{pi} — one flat transcript per pi process; no SubagentCounter, no parent-child linkage in the adapter
subagent-orphan-cleanup primitive is orphan-detection framed on parent+child session trees; 'Not to be confused with' separates it from single-session SIGKILL/session-end
